items.name: Senolytics coverage repair assessment
items.summary: The senolytics track has an auditable vertical slice across preclinical D+Q discovery/healthspan evidence, early human biomarker and pilot human studies, a phase 2 randomized bone trial, a 2025 cognition/mobility pilot, translation-review context, risk-of-bias records, and registry-only active trial-watch records. D+Q bone safety extraction now includes registry aggregates, full-text publication-table preferred-term rows beyond headache, and registry/article discontinuation fields; DKD, IPF, and AD-risk records now cite retained PubMed source snapshots for abstract-level effect and safety details, with the legacy IPF tolerability result reconciled to the N=12 randomized pilot. Registry surveillance remains incomplete because ClinicalTrials.gov snapshots for deferred fisetin frailty registries could not be fetched in the worker, and the review landscape remains incomplete because no systematic-review or meta-analysis source was screened. Full article/table safety and efficacy extraction for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone supplement material remains incomplete.

evidence_categories.rationale: Includes early D+Q human biomarker, randomized IPF pilot, phase 2 bone, and AD-risk cognition/mobility pilot records; DKD, IPF, and AD-risk now have at least abstract-located extraction for sample, dosing, timing, or effect details.
Includes foundational discovery work plus the old-mouse healthspan and post-treatment survival paper.
Cellular senescence targeting is anchored by the discovery source and translation review, but pathway-level extraction is not yet complete.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Future extraction should separate senescent-cell survival pathways, SASP markers, and tissue-specific clearance evidence.

evidence_categories.rationale: The IPF, bone, and AD-risk records explicitly encode mixed, pilot, primary/subgroup, and feasibility limitations instead of flattening them into positive claims; the AD-risk record now includes the abstract-level MoCA and TNF-alpha estimates.

Safety-oriented records now include the D+Q bone registry aggregate counts, full-text adverse-event table extraction, structured preferred-term rows for headache, diarrhea, nausea, constipation, gastrointestinal reflux, vertigo, fatigue, and body pain, and registry/article discontinuation fields. IPF and AD-risk safety slices now cite PubMed source snapshots for abstract-level safety statements, and DKD now has a source-snapshot-backed abstract limitation record. Cross-human safety remains thin because DKD, IPF, and AD-risk still need full article/table safety extraction and harmonized event definitions.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Run full article/table extraction for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk safety details, reconcile D+Q bone supplement safety material if available, and keep cross-human safety synthesis blocked until term definitions, denominators, and relatedness/seriousness fields are harmonized.

evidence_categories.rationale: Two active no-results trial-watch records were added, but the full senolytics registry surface still needs a broader surveillance pass.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Add deferred fisetin frailty registries and associated no-results status records in the next surveillance pass.

evidence_categories.rationale: The current slice includes a narrative translation review but no systematic-review or meta-analysis record.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Search specifically for recent systematic reviews, trial reviews, and evidence maps.

evidence_categories.rationale: Records separate senolytic mechanism/intervention class from evidence tier, clinical indication, and registry status, reducing the risk that the track becomes an unsupported efficacy bucket.

known_gaps.description: DKD, IPF, and AD-risk human studies now have retained PubMed source snapshots supporting abstract-level sample, dosing, timepoint, effect, and safety details, and the legacy IPF generic feasibility/tolerability extraction has been reconciled to the N=12 randomized pilot. Full article/table extraction for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone supplements remains incomplete.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Retain or ingest full-text/table source snapshots for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, and D+Q bone supplements, then reconcile abstract-level extraction against article tables before formal synthesis.

known_gaps.description: Aggregate adverse-event counts, the D+Q bone headache preferred-term row, additional publication-table preferred-term rows, and registry/article discontinuation fields have been extracted for the D+Q bone RCT. IPF and AD-risk now have PubMed source-snapshot-backed abstract safety extraction, and DKD has a source-snapshot-backed record documenting that adverse-event counts were not reported in the retained abstract summary. Full article/table safety extraction for DKD, IPF, AD-risk, D+Q bone supplement material, and harmonized cross-human adverse-event definitions remain incomplete.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run retained full-text or table-row extraction for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk safety details, reconcile any D+Q bone supplement safety tables, and create a cross-human safety compatibility group only after event terms, denominators, zero semantics, relatedness, and seriousness are harmonized.

known_gaps.description: Only two active no-results registry records are present. The prior coverage-repair session also deferred fisetin frailty registry candidates NCT03430037 and NCT03675724, but this batch could not fetch ClinicalTrials.gov source snapshots for those registries, so broader current fisetin and D+Q trial surveillance remains incomplete.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run a source-snapshot-backed ClinicalTrials.gov surveillance pass for NCT03430037, NCT03675724, senolytics, D+Q, fisetin, and senescence-targeting terms; add no-results status records only when fetched registry snapshots support them.

known_gaps.description: No recent systematic-review or meta-analysis source has been screened into the knowledge base. This bounded batch did not close the gap because the job supplied no source row or context pack and PubMed/NLM retrieval was unavailable from the worker environment.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run a bounded search/screening job for recent systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and trial-landscape reviews; create or refresh PubMed source snapshots before upgrading any review source beyond screened or triage-summary maturity.

items.next_coverage_action: Run source-snapshot-backed ClinicalTrials.gov surveillance for deferred fisetin frailty registries NCT03430037 and NCT03675724, run a dedicated review-landscape search/screening pass, create retained full-text or table-row extraction passes for DKD, IPF, and AD-risk safety and efficacy details, reconcile any D+Q bone supplement safety material, and keep cross-human safety synthesis blocked until event definitions, denominators, zero semantics, relatedness, and seriousness are harmonized.
consumer_warnings: Coverage is not synthesis-ready; use as a scoped evidence map or work queue, not as a pooled estimate basis.
High-priority gaps remain; downstream claims should preserve the known-gap context.

items.name: Senolytics Scaffold Coverage Assessment
items.summary: This assessment only proves the new repository scaffold can represent a source-backed senolytics evidence slice. It is not a full coverage assessment for the track.

evidence_categories.rationale: One foundational senolytic discovery source has been represented, but the broader seminal anchor set is not yet audited.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Run a real coverage-repair pass before treating the senolytics work scope as source-complete.

evidence_categories.rationale: No human senolytic trials have been ported into this scaffold yet.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Human D+Q, fisetin, and adjacent direct-senolytic trial records should be screened in the first real bootstrap or repair pass.

evidence_categories.rationale: The stricter track taxonomy now records inclusion, exclusion, and boundary notes for senolytics, but neighboring tracks are not yet represented.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Add neighboring senomorphic and immune-clearance boundaries before broader classification work.

known_gaps.description: The scaffold contains one preclinical discovery source but no direct human senolytics evidence.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run a bounded senolytics coverage-repair pass to add human trial, registry, safety, and limiting-evidence records.

known_gaps.description: The scaffold has not yet implemented result extraction or synthesis compatibility rules.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Add result and synthesis schemas before attempting evidence pooling.

items.next_coverage_action: Use this scaffold to drive the first real senolytics coverage-repair pass.
consumer_warnings: Coverage is not synthesis-ready; use as a scoped evidence map or work queue, not as a pooled estimate basis.
High-priority gaps remain; downstream claims should preserve the known-gap context.
Superseded by senolytics-coverage-repair-2026-06-21 for this track/hallmark scope.
